channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump chooses TV's Dr. Oz to run Medicare and Medicaid, Wall Street exec Lutnick for Commerce
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday tapped Dr. Mehmet Oz, a former television talk show host and heart surgeon, to head the agency that oversees health insurance programs for millions of older, poor and disabled Americans.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Mountain lion rescued from tree outside California home
item.description: First responders rescued a young mountain lion from a tree after he wandered into the yard of an Orange County home.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, NewsNation

item.title: WWII soldier's remains back home after 80 years
item.description: "Thank goodness for the kindness of the German people for burying him intact and with dignity," Zarifian's obituary reads.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Brill, NewsNation

item.title: Foreign governments criticize Hong Kong's sentencing of 45 activists. Beijing defends it
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Forty-five prominent activists in Hong Kong were sentenced to jail for up to 10 years on Tuesday, sparking criticism from foreign governments and rights groups. Beijing defended the decisions.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Donald Trump to nominate Dr. Oz for Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump plans to nominate former Pennsylvania Senate nominee Dr. Mehmet Oz to serve as the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Administrator.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KREX 5

item.title: Prosecution rests in trial over death of Georgia nursing student Laken Riley
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — Georgia nursing student Laken Riley texted and called her mom as she headed out for a run to see whether she had time to chat — but then didn't respond to her mother's calls or increasingly frantic text messages. Riley called her mother at 9:03 a.m. on Feb.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: LA rushes to become sanctuary city prior to Trump presidency
item.description: The ordinance is widely expected to pass. Critics point out that sanctuary city status was not something ever decided on by voters.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, NewsNation

item.title: Nvidia rivals focus on building a different kind of chip to power AI products
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — Building the current crop of artificial intelligence chatbots has relied on specialized computer chips pioneered by Nvidia, which dominates the market and made itself the poster child of the AI boom.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Big Lots to close more stores in California
item.description: As it continues to move through the Chapter 11 bankruptcy process, Big Lots has announced plans to close additional stores in California and elsewhere while seemingly pulling others out of the closure process.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Survivor of deadly trip across Canada-US border expected to testify in human smuggling trial
item.description: FERGUS FALLS, Minn. (AP) — An Indian migrant who survived a deadly trek across the Canada-U.S. border in blizzard conditions is expected to testify Tuesday in the federal trial of two men accused of taking part in a sprawling human smuggling scheme that ultimately killed a family of four.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL GOLDBERG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former 'American Idol' contestant pleads guilty in deadly DUI crash
item.description: Former "American Idol" contestant Caleb Kennedy has pleaded guilty to driving under the influence during a deadly 2022 crash in South Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Robert Cox, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Apartment buildings wrap up $1B in HUD funds for lower utility bills and climate upgrades
item.description: Renters in affordable housing felt forgotten and left out of the nation's transition to clean energy. They lived in older buildings that had been repaired over the years but had leaky windows and old appliances that consumed a lot of energy. They didn't have solar power.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mom 9 months pregnant missing for a month, last seen at boyfriend's house, family says
item.description: Pregnant mom Emma Baum, 25, went missing on Oct. 10 in Gary, Indiana, and her boyfriend is in custody on an unrelated warrant, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reuters hires former Post and AP executive editor Sally Buzbee to lead U.S., Canada news operation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sally Buzbee, the former executive editor of The Washington Post and The Associated Press, is joining Reuters as the news agency's top editor for the United States and Canada, the company said Tuesday. As news editor for the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hacker gains access to testimony in Gaetz investigations: reports
item.description: A hacker gained access to witness testimony related to investigations into former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), who resigned from Congress last week after President-elect Trump tapped him for attorney general, multiple news outlets reported Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Zilbermints, NewsNation

item.title: Mississippi restaurant, co-owner sentenced for decades-long fish mislabeling scheme
item.description: GULFPORT, Miss. (WJTV) - A Mississippi corporation operating as Mary Mahoney’s Old French House restaurant in Biloxi, Mississippi, and its co-owner and manager were sentenced on charges for a long-standing conspiracy to misbrand seafood.
item.dc:creator: Sethanie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: The White House's Christmas tree is a symbol of resilience for hurricane-hit North Carolina farms
item.description: NEWLAND, N.C. (AP) — The Cartner family had known since last year that one of their farm’s Christmas trees would be headed to the White House this winter.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3 killed in series of random stabbings in New York City
item.description: A man is accused of fatally stabbing three people across a swath of Manhattan on Monday morning, carrying out a series of random attacks without uttering a word to his victims, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: LIVE: SpaceX launches Starship rocket for sixth test flight
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is expected to attend the launch of a SpaceX rocket in Texas on Tuesday, sources familiar with the plans confirmed, in the latest sign of Trump’s closeness to the company's billionaire CEO Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: When Trump left office, students were struggling because of the pandemic. They still are
item.description: When President-elect Donald Trump left office in early 2021, the pandemic was at its height. Now he returns as American children and schools are struggling to overcome its lingering impact.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Gov Newsom announces decision in Menendez brothers case
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom says he will respect the will of the voters after they replaced the Los Angeles district attorney who made a last-minute push in case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suzanne Simpson's home may be sold as missing realtor's husband remains behind bars in murder case
item.description: Suzanne Simpson mysteriously disappeared over six weeks ago, and as her husband remains behind bars for her murder, her family home may now reportedly be sold.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 people are killed in random stabbings in New York City. A suspect is in custody
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A man fatally stabbed three people across a swath of Manhattan on Monday morning, carrying out a series of random attacks without uttering a word to his victims, officials said.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 hospitalized after California theme park riders stranded mid-air
item.description: Two thrill-seekers were hospitalized Monday after they became stranded for more than two hours on a malfunctioning ride at Knott’s Berry Farm in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: 2 women tell Ethics panel Matt Gaetz paid them for sex, attorney says
item.description: Leppard said the House Ethics Committee and his clients went one by one through a series of Venmo transactions.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson's 'epic' weekend with Trump shows strengths and limits of his power
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There was House Speaker Mike Johnson walking behind President-elect Donald Trump's entourage into Saturday night's UFC fight at Madison Square Garden, his stature overcome by the enormity of the scene around him. And Johnson mugging with musicians Kid Rock and Jelly Roll.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Agent says GPS data puts Georgia student Laken Riley in same area as man accused in her death
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — An FBI special agent testified Monday that electronic location data seems to place Georgia nursing student Laken Riley and the man accused of killing her in the same wooded area at the time of her death.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police seek witnesses, cell phone video after gunfire during a parade in New Orleans kills 2
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Deadly shootings at a signature New Orleans cultural event — a neighborhood “second-line” parade — left authorities pleading with witnesses to come forward with information or cell phone video while seeking to reassure the public that the weekend bloodshed was an aberration in a c
item.dc:creator: KEVIN McGILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 8-year-old who raised money for mother's headstone now giving back
item.description: Emouree Johnson, 8, ran a lemonade stand earlier this year to raise money for a headstone for her mother. Now, she's raising funds to help other kids.
item.dc:creator: Josiah Elmore, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signals use of military for deportations
item.description: President-elect Trump recirculated a social media post Monday suggesting his incoming administration will declare an immigration national emergency and use military assets to support his mass deportation pledge, labeling the claim “TRUE!!!” On his Truth Social site, Trump at 4:03 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden authorizes Ukraine to use US-supplied longer range missiles for deeper strikes inside Russia
item.description: MANAUS, Brazil (AP) — President Joe Biden has authorized Ukraine to use U.S.-supplied missiles to strike deeper inside Russia, easing limitations on the longer range weapons as Russia deploys thousands of North Korean troops to reinforce its war, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, COLLEEN LONG, ZEKE MILLER, MATTHEW LEE and ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Longest-waiting child in Arkansas foster care system finds forever family
item.description: After more than 6,100 days in the Arkansas foster care system, 17-year-old Jose has now found his forever family.
item.dc:creator: Mattison Gafner, KTLA 5

item.title: 2 killed, 10 wounded in shootings near New Orleans parade route
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Two people were killed and 10 others were wounded in two separate shootings along a New Orleans parade route and celebration attended by thousands on Sunday, authorities said. There were no immediate arrests. Officers responding to reports of gunfire shortly after 3:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FBI investigates new wave of offensive messages targeting Hispanic, LGBTQ people
item.description: The announcement comes days after African American students received similar messages stating they’d been selected to "pick cotton.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KTLA 5

item.title: US students who box, skydive and help youth and asylum seekers are among 2025's Rhodes scholars
item.description: A group of 32 students from the United States have been selected to attend the University of Oxford as part of the prestigious Rhodes scholar program in the coming year among an international class representing more than 70 nations, scholarship officials announced.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN P. D. HANNON and JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Need credit card debt relief? Debt management could help
item.description: If you can’t get favorable terms through a hardship plan or your issuer doesn’t offer this assistance, there’s still an option to consider.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Lambarena, KTLA 5

item.title: President of Kentucky plant that exploded says it is 'accountable' for neighborhood damages
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — The president of a Kentucky plant that exploded earlier this week said the company is accountable for the accident and will pay damages to residents affected by the blast.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, ABC 4
